holdbuffer.v
Authors
JAY CONVERTINO
Dates
2025/12/16
Information
Brief

Holdbuffer is used to pipeline backpressure busses.

holdbuffer
module holdbuffer #(
parameter 
integer 
BUS_WIDTH
 = 
8,
parameter 
integer 
ACK_ENABLE
 = 
0
)
(
input 
wire
clk,
input 
wire
rstn,
input 
wire
timeout,
input 
wire
enable,
input 
wire
 [BUS_WIDTH-1:0] 
s_data,
input 
wire
s_data_last,
input 
wire
s_data_valid,
output 
wire
s_data_ready,
output 
wire
s_data_ack,
output 
wire
 [BUS_WIDTH-1:0] 
m_data,
output 
wire
m_data_last,
output 
wire
m_data_valid,
input 
wire
m_data_ready,
input 
wire
m_data_ack
)

Hold data till ready

Parametes
BUS_WIDTH

width of the parallel data input in bits.

ACK_ENABLE

enable ack output of all data writes, and reads.

Ports
clk
input wire

global clock for the core.

rstn
input wire

negative syncronus reset to clk.

timeout
input wire

Active high to force core out of the hold state.

enable
input wire

Active high to allow output of data. When low all output is blocked.

s_data
input wire [BUS_WIDTH- 1:0]

Input data that is BUS_WIDTH bits wide.

s_data_last
input wire

Input data has hit the end of a stream of data.

s_data_valid
input wire

Input data is valid when 1 (high).

s_data_ready
output wire

Core is ready for input data when 1 (high).

s_data_ack
output wire

Output 1 when data is written to the register(s) of the core.

m_data
output wire [BUS_WIDTH- 1:0]

Output data that is BUS_WIDTH bits wide.

m_data_last
output wire

Output data has hit the end of a stream of data.

m_data_valid
output wire

Output data is valid when 1 (high).

m_data_ready
input wire

Downstream is ready to output data when 1 (high).

m_data_ack
input wire

Downstream has registered the valid data when 1 (high).

States

Core has 3 states, that includes an error state.

<GET>

d1

<HOLD>

d3

<ERROR>

d0
